summary: Proof-worth registry entries owned by runtime-readiness.

entries.contract: Content-root wait success, parser rejection, autostart, and missing-root timeout shape stay bounded.
entries.worth: This protects the canonical content wait surface without touching the shared repo daemon.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT

entries.contract: Linked git worktrees cannot use the default repo runtime without an explicit isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT.
entries.worth: This protects the single-checkout runtime ownership model from alternate-checkout agent work.
entries.guard: creates a disposable linked worktree and does not execute its native ./aos binary

entries.contract: Content wait polling does not accumulate socket listeners while status is unresolved.
entries.worth: This catches listener leaks with a fake socket instead of a live daemon loop.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Show wait bounds daemon read stalls by caller timeout and emits structured pending-condition JSON.
entries.worth: This proves timeout behavior without needing a real canvas or live repo daemon.
entries.guard: none

entries.contract: Show wait observes a URL-backed toolkit canvas readiness predicate through an isolated daemon.
entries.worth: This is the focused live smoke for the user-visible wait path without sharing the repo runtime.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT

entries.contract: Live-start operations fail closed without explicit allow-start authority.
entries.worth: This keeps content wait, show wait, launch, and experience activation from mutating runtime state accidentally.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT and asserts live start is blocked

entries.contract: Ready, status, doctor, and clean classify stale daemons consistently.
entries.worth: This keeps readiness proof bounded to mock and temporary daemon state instead of broad live cleanup.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT plus temporary mock processes

entries.contract: Readiness verdict composition, blocker priority, TCC staleness telemetry, and next-action ordering stay deterministic.
entries.worth: This catches readiness priority regressions without touching live TCC or daemon state.
entries.guard: model fixtures only; no live TCC mutation

entries.contract: Plain ready and post-permission verification execute no service actions, while explicit repair owns bounded cleanup, start, restart, and recovery.
entries.worth: This proves the public mutation boundary through fake service argv and isolated runtime state instead of relying on source shape.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT, mock daemons, and fake service actions

entries.contract: Daemon/CLI permission disagreement remains fail-closed, plain readiness stays side-effect free, and explicit repair owns the one-shot stale-TCC alert.
entries.worth: This protects the permission-loss and post-rebuild handoff boundary with a mock daemon instead of live TCC mutation.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT, mock daemon health, fake repair service actions, and no live TCC mutation

entries.contract: Permission setup markers remain identity-aware across worktrees without bypassing missing-marker readiness blockers.
entries.worth: This prevents marker-path provenance from silently weakening readiness setup requirements.
entries.guard: uses temporary worktree-shaped paths and mocked permission facts

entries.contract: Readiness reports daemon ownership mismatch without mutating the managed service and routes recovery through explicit repair.
entries.worth: This proves ownership priority and zero implicit service mutation against deterministic mock process facts.
entries.guard: uses isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT, mock daemon health, and fake launchd ownership facts

entries.contract: Private runtime and readiness broker primitives preserve their native acceptance scenario until the owning source route is available.
entries.worth: This prevents a future native acceptance proof from drifting silently while keeping it out of default no-build validation.
entries.guard: requires a current rebuilt native ./aos and an isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT; use bash -n for no-build edits

entries.contract: Daemon input-region routing retains typed destination and diagnostic metadata through posting, serializes an exact two-key envelope at the boundary, and clears capture while failing open when canonical delivery is impossible.
entries.worth: This protects native input from duplicate transport fields, silent consumption, and stranded capture without launching the shared daemon.
entries.guard: compiles shared Swift input models, executes deliver/fail-open capture behavior, and validates generated payloads; no live daemon or input tap

entries.contract: Lifecycle completion and native retirement remain generation-bound across same-ID replacement, partial abandonment, and deferred AppKit finalization.
entries.worth: This executes the identity and retirement transaction directly without relying on source-shape assertions or a shared daemon.
entries.guard: compiles the lifecycle coordinator with a minimal native resource and runs on the main AppKit loop; no shared daemon or input tap

entries.contract: Mouse, drag, key, typing, and modifier actions hold one exact terminal receipt expectation, ignore nonterminal observations, retain uncertain modifier transitions for session cleanup, keep acknowledged drag-down release transaction-owned until confirmed up, and expose public streaming session stdin without fixed timing delays.
entries.worth: The executable Swift model covers terminal sequencing, timeout teardown, expectation replacement, provisional or uncertain modifier state, and drag release obligation success/failure; static wiring and the public wrapper prove integration ownership.
entries.guard: executable transaction model plus static ownership and public-wrapper streaming; the guarded concurrent lane separately proves live click delivery

entries.contract: Interactive and passive canvas creation, lookup, and transactional retirement preserve daemon identity, coherent lifecycle state, and native-window ownership.
entries.worth: This deterministically catches lifecycle regressions and orphan native windows without requiring injected user input.
entries.guard: uses an isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT and requires a current raw native build

entries.contract: A successful public ten-second coordinate drag requires action-owned down and terminal-up acknowledgments, emits its receipt-tagged stream, and leaves the managed daemon input tap active and permission-capable.
entries.worth: This executes the long multi-event action that previously starved the action receipt run loop and verifies the managed daemon tap does not degrade or require service restoration afterward.
entries.guard: requires explicit AOS_REAL_INPUT_OK=1, native input permissions, a current raw build, and the live repo daemon; incidental user input is permitted

entries.contract: Canvas create, lookup, and retirement stay transactional while canonical pointer events continue through persistent v1 see.observe and a generation-bound harness canvas input_event subscription with ordered down/up delivery and bounded event-to-JS latency.
entries.worth: This reproduces the AppKit/WebKit release race class while exercising both lossless socket observation and the live global-tap-to-WebKit fanout path.
entries.guard: requires explicit AOS_REAL_INPUT_OK=1, native input permissions, and an isolated AOS_STATE_ROOT; incidental user mouse or keyboard input is permitted

entries.contract: The wait/surface housecleaning report remains an evidence pointer, not a deletion authority by itself.
entries.worth: This protects test cleanup decisions from deleting a proof before its replacement command is green and routed.
entries.guard: report-only; run the named replacement proof before deleting a test

entries.contract: The test harness ladder prep report remains an evidence pointer for harness selection, not a standing command source.
entries.worth: This keeps harness cleanup and promotion decisions tied to current routed proofs instead of stale report prose.
entries.guard: report-only; use current workflow routing before adding, moving, or deleting test helpers
